response:
THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED AS FOLLOWS:  
THE FIRST CIRCLE HAS AN AREA OF πr², THE SECOND CIRCLE HAS AN AREA OF π(1.1r)², AND SO ON, UNTIL THE EIGHTH CIRCLE. THE TOTAL AREA IS πr² + π(1.1r)² + π(1.1²r)² + π(1.1³r)² + π(1.1⁴r)² + π(1.1⁵r)² + π(1.1⁶r)² + π(1.1⁷r)².  
THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE FIFTH CIRCLE IS 110. THIS IS DETERMINED BY DIVIDING THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE FIFTH CIRCLE BY THE GAP BETWEEN CHARACTERS, AND THEN ROUNDING DOWN TO THE NEAREST INTEGER.  
THE LETTER I APPEARS AT LEAST 11 TIMES IN THIS RESPONSE.
